sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act and to enact An Act to change the names of certain electoral districts, 2026
oldText:
Section 9 — Appropriate purposes.
(1) An organization may collect, use or disclose personal information only for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate in the circumstances.

Section 15 — Consent.
(3) Consent of an individual is required for the collection, use or disclosure of personal information, except as otherwise provided.

Section 39 — Disclosure without consent.
(1) An organization may disclose an individual's personal information without their knowledge or consent only in the circumstances expressly set out in this Act.
updatedText:
Section 9 — Appropriate purposes.
(1) An organization may collect, use or disclose personal information only in a manner and for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate in the circumstances, having regard to (a) the sensitivity of the personal information; (b) whether the purposes represent legitimate business needs of the organization; (c) the effectiveness of the collection, use or disclosure in meeting the organization's legitimate business needs; (d) whether there are less intrusive means of achieving those purposes at a comparable cost and with comparable benefits; and (e) whether the individual's loss of privacy is proportionate to the benefits.

Section 15 — Valid consent.
(3) The consent of an individual is valid only if it is reasonable to expect that the individual would understand the nature, purpose and consequences of the collection, use or disclosure of the personal information to which they are consenting.

Section 39 — Disclosure for socially beneficial purposes.
(1) An organization may disclose an individual's de-identified personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution, a health care institution, a post-secondary educational institution or public library in Canada, or any other prescribed entity, if the disclosure is made for a socially beneficial purpose.
deltaSummary: C-27 introduces a five-factor reasonable-purposes test, narrows consent to 'valid consent', and adds a socially beneficial disclosure pathway for de-identified personal information.
detailedDelta: Section 9(1) is replaced with a factor-based appropriateness test (sensitivity, legitimate business need, effectiveness, less intrusive alternatives, proportionality). Section 15(3) replaces 'consent' with 'valid consent', requiring that the individual could reasonably understand the nature, purpose and consequences. New s. 39(1) creates a permitted disclosure of de-identified personal information to government, health, post-secondary, library, or prescribed entities for socially beneficial purposes.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council, no earlier than one year after Royal Assent.
humanReviewReason: Confidence below 0.75; effective date depends on order in council; coming-into-force language requires lawyer verification.

sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act
oldText:
Section 3 — Sale of feeds.
No person shall sell, offer for sale or have in possession for sale any feed unless the feed conforms to the prescribed standards and is registered as prescribed.
updatedText:
Section 3 — Sale of feeds.
No person shall sell, offer for sale or have in possession for sale any feed unless the feed conforms to the prescribed standards, is registered as prescribed, and bears a label disclosing every novel ingredient and any genetically modified component, in plain language and in both official languages.
deltaSummary: C-273 adds a plain-language, bilingual labelling obligation for novel and genetically modified feed ingredients on every retail unit.
detailedDelta: The existing s. 3 sale prohibition is extended with a labelling requirement covering novel ingredients and any genetically modified component. Labels must be plain-language and bilingual.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act
oldText:
Section 3 — Sale.
No person shall sell or import into Canada any fertilizer or supplement unless the fertilizer or supplement conforms to the prescribed standards and is registered.
updatedText:
Section 3 — Sale.
No person shall sell or import into Canada any fertilizer or supplement unless the fertilizer or supplement conforms to the prescribed standards, is registered, and is accompanied by a publicly accessible disclosure of its source materials, processing aids and any genetically modified components.
deltaSummary: C-273 adds a public source-and-component disclosure obligation for every fertilizer and supplement sold or imported.
detailedDelta: Section 3 is extended with a public disclosure obligation covering source materials, processing aids, and genetically modified components. The disclosure must be publicly accessible.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
humanReviewReason: Regulation will define what 'publicly accessible' means in practice.

sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act
oldText:
Section 3 — Sale and import of seed.
No person shall sell, offer for sale or import into Canada any seed unless the seed conforms to the prescribed standards.
updatedText:
Section 3 — Sale and import of seed.
No person shall sell, offer for sale or import into Canada any seed unless the seed conforms to the prescribed standards and is identified, on the package and in any sales record, as either conventional, hybrid or genetically modified.
deltaSummary: C-273 requires every seed package and sales record to identify the seed as conventional, hybrid or genetically modified.
detailedDelta: Section 3 now requires a categorical disclosure (conventional / hybrid / genetically modified) on every seed package and in every sales record.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act
oldText:
Section 6 — Prohibitions.
(1) No person shall manufacture, possess, handle, store, transport, import, distribute or use a pest control product that is not registered under this Act, except as authorized under this Act or the regulations.
updatedText:
Section 6 — Prohibitions.
(1) No person shall manufacture, possess, handle, store, transport, import, distribute or use a pest control product that is not registered under this Act, and every registered pest control product shall display, on the consumer-facing label, every active ingredient and the residue tolerance applicable to its principal authorized use, except as authorized under this Act or the regulations.
deltaSummary: C-273 layers an active-ingredient and residue-tolerance disclosure onto the consumer-facing label of every registered pest control product.
detailedDelta: Section 6(1) is extended: registered pest control products must display every active ingredient and the residue tolerance for their principal authorized use on the consumer-facing label.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
humanReviewReason: Confidence below 0.75; 'principal authorized use' is undefined and needs lawyer interpretation.

sourceBillTitle: An Act to amend the Feeds Act, the Fertilizers Act, the Seeds Act, the Pest Control Products Act and the Food and Drugs Act
oldText:
Section 5 — Misleading labelling.
(1) No person shall label, package, treat, process, sell or advertise any food in a manner that is false, misleading or deceptive or is likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its character, value, quantity, composition, merit or safety.
updatedText:
Section 5 — Misleading labelling.
(1) No person shall label, package, treat, process, sell or advertise any food in a manner that is false, misleading or deceptive or is likely to create an erroneous impression regarding its character, value, quantity, composition, merit or safety, and any food derived from genetically modified ingredients shall be conspicuously identified as such on the principal display panel.
deltaSummary: C-273 layers a conspicuous GM-derived identification onto the principal display panel of any food derived from genetically modified ingredients.
detailedDelta: Section 5(1) of the Food and Drugs Act is extended: foods derived from genetically modified ingredients must carry a conspicuous identification on the principal display panel.
comingIntoForceText: On a day fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
